imported.question: The provisions of the Public Service Rules (PSR) shall be enforced by:
options: All Ministries, Extra-Ministerial Offices and Agencies
The Head of the Civil Service of the Federation
imported.explanation: Correct option: B (All Ministries, Extra-Ministerial Offices and Agencies).

imported.question: The Public Service Rules apply to:
options: Only officers in the Federal Civil Service
All officers in the Public Service
All civil servants except where they conflict with specific terms in their contract
imported.explanation: Correct option: B (All officers in the Public Service).

imported.question: Which of the following offices is NOT explicitly mentioned in the rules?
imported.explanation: Correct option: D (Chief Justice of Nigeria).

imported.question: To what extent do these rules apply to the offices mentioned in 010102?
options: Only to the extent that they are not inconsistent with the Constitution
At the discretion of the officers
imported.explanation: Correct option: C (Only to the extent that they are not inconsistent with the Constitution).

imported.question: What is the duty of every officer regarding the Public Service Rules?
options: To be aware of some of the rules
To acquaint himself with the Public Service Rules and other extant regulations and circulars
To seek clarification from their superiors
imported.explanation: Correct option: B (To acquaint himself with the Public Service Rules and other extant regulations and circulars).

imported.question: Where are the special meanings of words and terms used in particular chapters or sections found?
options: At the end of the document
At the beginning of such chapters or sections
In the Federal Republic of Nigeria Official Gazette
imported.explanation: Correct option: C (At the beginning of such chapters or sections).

imported.question: What does "Accredited Representative of the Federal Republic of Nigeria" mean?
options: The Diplomatic or Consular Representative of the Federal Republic of Nigeria within the area
Any representative of the Nigerian government
A representative accredited to the United Nations
imported.explanation: Correct option: B (The Diplomatic or Consular Representative of the Federal Republic of Nigeria within the area).

imported.question: What does "Civil Service of the Federation" mean?
options: Service of the Federation in the civil capacity as a staff of the Office of the President, Vice President, a Ministry and Extra Ministerial Offices of the Government of the Federation assigned with the responsibility for any business of the Government of the Federation. It is a body or organ which enjoys continuity of existence.
Contractual service with the Federal Government
imported.explanation: Correct option: B (Service of the Federation in the civil capacity as a staff of the Office of the President, Vice President, a Ministry and Extra Ministerial Offices of the Government of the Federation assigned with the responsibility for any business of the Government of the Federation. It is a body or organ which enjoys continuity of existence.).

imported.question: "Classified Correspondence" is correspondence that has been graded:
options: Restricted, Confidential, Secret or Top Secret
imported.explanation: Correct option: C (Restricted, Confidential, Secret or Top Secret).

imported.question: What does "Cut-off Mark" mean?
options: The lowest score on an examination
Examination score set by the Federal Civil Service Commission to determine eligible candidates for promotion among those who pass the examination as determined by declared vacancies.
imported.explanation: Correct option: C (Examination score set by the Federal Civil Service Commission to determine eligible candidates for promotion among those who pass the examination as determined by declared vacancies.).

imported.question: What is an "Established Post"?
options: A post provided for under the Personnel Emoluments sub-head of the Estimates.
A post in any of the Public Service of the Federal Republic of Nigeria.
imported.explanation: Correct option: B (A post provided for under the Personnel Emoluments sub-head of the Estimates.).

imported.question: Who is an "Expatriate Officer"?
options: An officer from another African country
A staff who ranks as such for the purpose of pension in accordance with the Pension Reform Act 2004 as amended.
imported.explanation: Correct option: C (A staff who ranks as such for the purpose of pension in accordance with the Pension Reform Act 2004 as amended.).

imported.question: What does "Gazette" mean?
options: The Federal Republic of Nigeria Official Gazette.
imported.explanation: Correct option: B (The Federal Republic of Nigeria Official Gazette.).

imported.question: What does "Interdiction" mean?
options: The temporary removal of an officer from performing his normal duties when disciplinary proceedings for his dismissal are being undertaken or is about to be undertaken and shall be placed on half salary pending the determination of the case.
imported.explanation: Correct option: B (The temporary removal of an officer from performing his normal duties when disciplinary proceedings for his dismissal are being undertaken or is about to be undertaken and shall be placed on half salary pending the determination of the case.).

imported.question: Who is a "Junior Officer"?
options: An officer on GL.08 and below
A pensionable officer on GL.06 and below and their equivalent.
Any officer below the rank of director
imported.explanation: Correct option: B (A pensionable officer on GL.06 and below and their equivalent.).

imported.question: What are "Junior Posts"?
options: Posts in Ministries/Extra-Ministerial Offices attracting emoluments on Grade Level 06 and below and their equivalent.
imported.explanation: Correct option: A (Posts in Ministries/Extra-Ministerial Offices attracting emoluments on Grade Level 06 and below and their equivalent.).

imported.question: What does "Monthly-Rated" mean?
options: Employment on monthly rates of pay.
imported.explanation: Correct option: C (Employment on monthly rates of pay.).

imported.question: What is "Nigerian Home Place"?
options: The place in Nigeria accepted in writing by Government as the place to which a staff would naturally wish to return when spending his leave in Nigeria.
imported.explanation: Correct option: B (The place in Nigeria accepted in writing by Government as the place to which a staff would naturally wish to return when spending his leave in Nigeria.).

imported.question: Who is a "Nigerian Officer"?
options: An officer other than an expatriate.
An officer from a specific region
imported.explanation: Correct option: B (An officer other than an expatriate.).

imported.question: What does "Officer" mean when used without qualification?
options: Staff in an established post, either on pensionable or contract terms.
imported.explanation: Correct option: B (Staff in an established post, either on pensionable or contract terms.).

imported.question: What does "Pass Mark" mean?
options: The minimum score required for employment
The score below which a candidate is considered to have failed promotion examination which currently is 60%
imported.explanation: Correct option: C (The score below which a candidate is considered to have failed promotion examination which currently is 60%).

imported.question: Who is a "Permanent Secretary"?
options: Officer in charge of day-to-day administration in the Ministry/Extra-Ministerial Offices. He/she also doubles as the accounting officer and chief policy adviser to the Minister. They are appointed from among serving Directors in the Federal Civil Service.
imported.explanation: Correct option: B (Officer in charge of day-to-day administration in the Ministry/Extra-Ministerial Offices. He/she also doubles as the accounting officer and chief policy adviser to the Minister. They are appointed from among serving Directors in the Federal Civil Service.).

imported.question: What does "Public Service of the Federation" mean?
options: The service of the Federation in any capacity in respect of the Government of the Federation as defined in part 4 section 318 of 1999 Constitution (as amended).
imported.explanation: Correct option: B (The service of the Federation in any capacity in respect of the Government of the Federation as defined in part 4 section 318 of 1999 Constitution (as amended).).

imported.question: What do "Public Service Rules" consist of?
options: Policies and guidelines which define the employment relationship between Public Servants and the Government; they also spell out the Conditions of Service and Human Resources Management procedures.
imported.explanation: Correct option: A (Policies and guidelines which define the employment relationship between Public Servants and the Government; they also spell out the Conditions of Service and Human Resources Management procedures.).

imported.question: What is a "Scheme of Service"?
options: A welfare package for public servants

A reference document in the public service that specifies the duties of each post, method of entry, including qualification requirements and the prerequisites for advancement within and beyond cadres.
imported.explanation: Correct option: B (A reference document in the public service that specifies the duties of each post, method of entry, including qualification requirements and the prerequisites for advancement within and beyond cadres.).

imported.question: What are "Senior Posts"?
options: Posts attracting emoluments on Grade Level GL.07 and above and other posts attracting fixed emoluments of equivalent Grade Levels in Ministries/Extra-Ministerial Offices.
imported.explanation: Correct option: A (Posts attracting emoluments on Grade Level GL.07 and above and other posts attracting fixed emoluments of equivalent Grade Levels in Ministries/Extra-Ministerial Offices.).

imported.question: Who is a "Spouse of an Officer"?
options: A wife/husband married under the Marriage Act, Customary Law or Islamic Law.
Only a wife married under the Marriage Act
Only a husband married under Islamic Law
imported.explanation: Correct option: B (A wife/husband married under the Marriage Act, Customary Law or Islamic Law.).

imported.question: What does "Staff" mean?
options: A person employed by the Federal Government.
imported.explanation: Correct option: B (A person employed by the Federal Government.).

imported.question: What does "Suspension" mean?
options: Removal of an Officer from performing the duties of his office, where a prima facie case, of a serious nature has been established against the Officer, during which period he shall earn no salary.
imported.explanation: Correct option: B (Removal of an Officer from performing the duties of his office, where a prima facie case, of a serious nature has been established against the Officer, during which period he shall earn no salary.).

imported.question: Who is a "Trainee"?
options: A person appointed to a training post in any grade; it includes "pupil".
imported.explanation: Correct option: B (A person appointed to a training post in any grade; it includes "pupil".).

imported.question: How can the Federal Government amend the provisions of the Public Service Rules?
options: By issuing an appropriate Circular during the lifespan of a PSR.
Through the Head of Civil Service of the Federation
imported.explanation: Correct option: B (By issuing an appropriate Circular during the lifespan of a PSR.).

imported.question: How are the terms 'Officer' and 'Staff' referred to in the Public Service Rules to be applied in terms of gender?
options: The provisions of the Rules apply equally to both genders.
At the discretion of the Permanent Secretary
imported.explanation: Correct option: C (The provisions of the Rules apply equally to both genders.).

imported.question: What should a copy of the Public Service Rules form part of?
options: The inventory of items in all offices of the Public Service.
imported.explanation: Correct option: B (The inventory of items in all offices of the Public Service.).

imported.question: Appointments to offices in the Federal Civil Service are made on the authority of:
options: The Head of the Civil Service of the Federation
imported.explanation: Correct option: B (The Federal Civil Service Commission).

imported.question: How are appointments made?
options: Either by letter written under the direction of the Federal Civil Service Commission or by formal agreement.
imported.explanation: Correct option: C (Either by letter written under the direction of the Federal Civil Service Commission or by formal agreement.).

imported.question: Who is authorized to appoint eligible candidates to posts in respect of which the powers of appointment have been delegated to them?
options: The Head of the Civil Service of the Federation
imported.explanation: Correct option: C (Permanent Secretaries/Heads of Extra-Ministerial Offices).

imported.question: The Federal Civil Service Commission shall make appointments to posts graded:
imported.explanation: Correct option: B (GL.07-17).

imported.question: How shall appointments by the FCSC be made?
options: As the need arises into the available vacancies after advertisement.
imported.explanation: Correct option: A (As the need arises into the available vacancies after advertisement.).

imported.question: Where shall advertisement for vacancies be placed?
options: In three national newspapers and the Commission's website.
imported.explanation: Correct option: C (In three national newspapers and the Commission's website.).

imported.question: What is the deadline for submission of applications from interested candidates?
options: Six weeks from the date of placement of the advertisement.
imported.explanation: Correct option: C (Six weeks from the date of placement of the advertisement.).

imported.question: Who shall select from the pool of successful candidates at the Civil Service Examination?
imported.explanation: Correct option: B (Each Ministry/Extra-Ministerial Office).

imported.question: Who handles the selection process in each Ministry?
options: The appropriate Committees of each Ministry with representatives of the FCSC and OHCSF.
imported.explanation: Correct option: B (The appropriate Committees of each Ministry with representatives of the FCSC and OHCSF.).

imported.question: How often is the competitive Public/Civil Service Entry Examination held for posts graded GL. 07-10?
imported.explanation: Correct option: B (Annually).

imported.question: Who conducts the Public/Civil Service Entry Examination?
options: FCSC in conjunction with OHCSF, ASCON and PSIN.
imported.explanation: Correct option: B (FCSC in conjunction with OHCSF, ASCON and PSIN.).

imported.question: What is the qualification requirement for the Public/Civil Service Entry Examination?
options: As provided for in the approved Schemes of Service.
imported.explanation: Correct option: B (As provided for in the approved Schemes of Service.).

imported.question: Who makes the final recommendation for appointments?
options: The line Ministries/Extra-Ministerial Offices in line with their requirements.
imported.explanation: Correct option: C (The line Ministries/Extra-Ministerial Offices in line with their requirements.).

imported.question: Who appoints Junior Staff on GL. 06 and below?
options: Junior Staff Committee of each Ministry/Office.
imported.explanation: Correct option: D (Junior Staff Committee of each Ministry/Office.).

imported.question: How are appointments in Federal Offices located in the States/Zones made?
options: From the residents of those States/Zones who possess the qualifications.
From other states if no local candidates qualify
imported.explanation: Correct option: B (From the residents of those States/Zones who possess the qualifications.).

imported.question: Who works out the actual establishment for each State Office?
imported.explanation: Correct option: C (OHCSF.).

imported.question: Who conducts the interview to appoint the Officers (Junior Staff)?
imported.explanation: Correct option: C (Junior Staff Committee (Local).).

imported.question: Who approves the appointment of Junior Staff?
options: The Permanent Secretary/Head of Extra-Ministerial Offices.
imported.explanation: Correct option: C (The Permanent Secretary/Head of Extra-Ministerial Offices.).

imported.question: When is the incremental date of an Officer appointed or promoted to a post?
imported.explanation: Correct option: C (1st January of every year.).

imported.question: What is the condition for an Officer to enjoy the increment?
options: Spending at least six (6) months on the Grade with good performance.
imported.explanation: Correct option: B (Spending at least six (6) months on the Grade with good performance.).

imported.question: How is seniority in any Department determined?
options: By the entry date/assumption of duty as reflected in the register and career progression.
imported.explanation: Correct option: C (By the entry date/assumption of duty as reflected in the register and career progression.).

imported.question: Is the employment of unpaid Staff allowed?
options: No, the employment of unpaid Staff is prohibited.
imported.explanation: Correct option: C (No, the employment of unpaid Staff is prohibited.).

imported.question: Who is responsible for keeping an up-to-date record of every Officer under their control?
imported.explanation: Correct option: C (Permanent Secretaries/Head of Extra-Ministerial Offices.).

imported.question: Which form shall be used for staff records?
imported.explanation: Correct option: C (Staff Record Form (Gen 60).).

imported.question: Where must a copy of the Staff Record Form (Gen 60) be sent?
options: National Record Centre (OHCSF), Accountant-General, Auditor-General, and PenCom.
imported.explanation: Correct option: B (National Record Centre (OHCSF), Accountant-General, Auditor-General, and PenCom.).

imported.question: Within what time frame should the Staff Record Form (Gen 60) be sent?
options: Within one month of the appointment.
imported.explanation: Correct option: C (Within one month of the appointment.).

imported.question: How should Form Gen 60 be completed?
imported.explanation: Correct option: C (In ink.).

imported.question: Can the date of birth recorded on appointment be changed?
options: No, date of birth recorded on appointment shall not be changed throughout the career.
imported.explanation: Correct option: C (No, date of birth recorded on appointment shall not be changed throughout the career.).

imported.question: What is the contravention of Rule 020107 regarding the date of birth regarded as?
imported.explanation: Correct option: C (An act of serious misconduct.).

imported.question: What does "Recruitment" mean?
options: Filling of vacancies by direct appointment of persons not already in any Public Service of Nigeria.
imported.explanation: Correct option: C (Filling of vacancies by direct appointment of persons not already in any Public Service of Nigeria.).

imported.question: Which of the following is a category of appointment to the Federal Public Service?
imported.explanation: Correct option: D (Lateral entry.).

imported.question: Appointments made by the FCSC to NYSC Presidential Merit Award winners are:
imported.explanation: Correct option: B (Automatic regardless of vacancy constraints.).

imported.question: What percentage of recruitments shall be for persons with disability?
imported.explanation: Correct option: C (5%.).

imported.question: Where posts prove difficult to fill from within the Service, they shall normally be:
imported.explanation: Correct option: C (Advertised.).

imported.question: What type of appointment may be given to a candidate who requires additional experience?
imported.explanation: Correct option: C (Trainee/Pupil.).

imported.question: What is the normal service period for a trainee or pupil?
imported.explanation: Correct option: C (Two years.).

imported.question: Under what condition may the trainee period be reduced?
options: This period may however, be reduced in special circumstances.
imported.explanation: Correct option: B (This period may however, be reduced in special circumstances.).

imported.question: What happens after the completion of the "trainee period"?
options: The pupil may be appointed to the full grade on probation.
imported.explanation: Correct option: B (The pupil may be appointed to the full grade on probation.).

imported.question: How is a confirmed Officer treated when advanced to a training grade?
options: As if he had been seconded thereto.
imported.explanation: Correct option: B (As if he had been seconded thereto.).

imported.question: What is the normal maximum period of secondment for training?
options: The period laid down for the training except with OHCSF special advice.
imported.explanation: Correct option: B (The period laid down for the training except with OHCSF special advice.).

imported.question: All first appointments to pensionable posts other than trainee posts will be:
imported.explanation: Correct option: B (On probation.).

imported.question: Is an Officer confirmed in a lower post regarded as on probation in a higher post to which he is converted?
imported.explanation: Correct option: B (No.).

imported.question: What is the minimum age for appointment into the Federal Public Service?
imported.explanation: Correct option: C (18 years.).

imported.question: What is the maximum age for appointment into the Federal Public Service?
imported.explanation: Correct option: B (50 years.).

imported.question: What is one of the qualifications every applicant must possess?
imported.explanation: Correct option: A (Computer proficiency/literacy.).

imported.question: Who must certify an applicant as medically fit?
imported.explanation: Correct option: B (An authorized Healthcare Provider.).

imported.question: What kind of testimonial is required from applicants?
options: Good conduct from last employer or last school.
imported.explanation: Correct option: A (Good conduct from last employer or last school.).

imported.question: Where are the requisite qualifications for appointment provided?
imported.explanation: Correct option: C (Schemes of Service.).

imported.question: What is required for an Officer to be appointed?
options: Authorized establishment and recruitment waiver from OHCSF.
imported.explanation: Correct option: A (Authorized establishment and recruitment waiver from OHCSF.).

imported.question: In which case is prior specific approval required for an appointment?
options: If candidate has been convicted of a criminal offence.
imported.explanation: Correct option: A (If candidate has been convicted of a criminal offence.).

imported.question: If previously dismissed, what is required for a new appointment?
options: Prior specific approval of FCSC or Board/Council.
imported.explanation: Correct option: C (Prior specific approval of FCSC or Board/Council.).

imported.question: What must every applicant for government employment state?
options: Whether they have been convicted of a criminal offence.
imported.explanation: Correct option: B (Whether they have been convicted of a criminal offence.).

imported.question: What must an applicant state about previous employment?
options: All employment they have engaged in.
imported.explanation: Correct option: B (All employment they have engaged in.).

imported.question: What must an applicant disclose if they have left any employment?
imported.explanation: Correct option: B (Why they did so.).

imported.question: What must an applicant state if they are still in any employment?
options: Whether they are under any obligation to remain in it.
imported.explanation: Correct option: B (Whether they are under any obligation to remain in it.).

imported.question: What must an applicant declare regarding financial status?
options: Whether they are free from financial embarrassment.
imported.explanation: Correct option: B (Whether they are free from financial embarrassment.).

imported.question: Which form is to be completed by applicants for senior posts?
imported.explanation: Correct option: B (FCSC Application Form (FC.2).).

imported.question: Who processes FC.2 forms and publishes shortlists?
imported.explanation: Correct option: C (The Commission (FCSC).).

imported.question: Which form shall accompany Gen. 69C?
imported.explanation: Correct option: C (Form No. Gen. 60.).

imported.question: When shall an Officer enter Personal Particulars on Gen. 60?
options: Immediately a newly appointed Officer assumes duty.
imported.explanation: Correct option: B (Immediately a newly appointed Officer assumes duty.).

imported.question: What is the duty of every Permanent Secretary regarding the Oath of Secrecy?
options: Ensure all newly employed sign an Oath of Secrecy (Form 1) and that Oaths are preserved.
imported.explanation: Correct option: B (Ensure all newly employed sign an Oath of Secrecy (Form 1) and that Oaths are preserved.).

imported.question: Does the Oath of Secrecy prejudice the Freedom of Information Act?
imported.explanation: Correct option: B (No.).

imported.question: Can a public Officer be a member of a secret society?
imported.explanation: Correct option: C (No.).

imported.question: What should an Officer who is a member of a secret society before appointment do?
options: Renounce membership forthwith by statutory declaration.
imported.explanation: Correct option: C (Renounce membership forthwith by statutory declaration.).

imported.question: What is the contravention of Rule 020211 (Secret Societies) regarded as?
imported.explanation: Correct option: C (An act of serious misconduct.).

imported.question: What disciplinary action may be taken for contravening Rule 020211?
imported.explanation: Correct option: C (Dismissal from the service.).

imported.question: How long are officers on probation required to serve?
imported.explanation: Correct option: B (Two years.).

imported.question: Under what condition may probation be reduced?
options: Deduction of previous satisfactory Public Service in cognate status.
imported.explanation: Correct option: B (Deduction of previous satisfactory Public Service in cognate status.).

imported.question: What is the maximum probationary period allowed without extension?
imported.explanation: Correct option: B (Three years.).

imported.question: What may result from an extension of probation?
imported.explanation: Correct option: C (Incremental penalty (Rule 040206).).

imported.question: What is an Officer required to do during probation?
options: Pass the prescribed examination appropriate to appointment.
imported.explanation: Correct option: B (Pass the prescribed examination appropriate to appointment.).

imported.question: For senior posts, what do prescribed examinations include?
options: Those described in Chapter 7 and individual offer of appointment.
imported.explanation: Correct option: B (Those described in Chapter 7 and individual offer of appointment.).

imported.question: For clerical grade Officers, what is the prescribed examination?
imported.explanation: Correct option: C (Promotion/Advancement Examination for clerical officers.).

imported.question: What happens if a clerical Officer fails the exam during probation?
imported.explanation: Correct option: B (Probation may be extended.).

imported.question: What happens if a clerical Officer fails the exam at the end of an extended probation?
imported.explanation: Correct option: B (Appointment will be terminated.).

imported.question: For professional cadres, what is the condition for confirmation?
options: Satisfactory completion of probation and possession of Scheme of Service qualifications.
imported.explanation: Correct option: B (Satisfactory completion of probation and possession of Scheme of Service qualifications.).

imported.question: Who is responsible for ensuring conditions for confirmation are met?
imported.explanation: Correct option: C (Permanent Secretary/Head of Extra-Ministerial Office.).

imported.question: When is an Officer eligible for confirmation?
options: After satisfactory completion of probation, if Head of Dept is satisfied with performance.
imported.explanation: Correct option: B (After satisfactory completion of probation, if Head of Dept is satisfied with performance.).

imported.question: Who conveys the confirmation to the Officer?
options: FCSC or Permanent Secretary as the case may be.
imported.explanation: Correct option: B (FCSC or Permanent Secretary as the case may be.).

imported.question: What happens if service during probation is unsatisfactory?
imported.explanation: Correct option: B (Appointment may be terminated.).

imported.question: Who can approve termination of an Officer on probation?
options: FCSC or Permanent Secretary as the case may be.
imported.explanation: Correct option: C (FCSC or Permanent Secretary as the case may be.).

imported.question: What is the general principle governing advancement and promotion?
imported.explanation: Correct option: B (Merit, experience, and ability.).

imported.question: What must an Officer possess to be eligible for promotion?
options: Satisfactory APERs for the three preceding years.
imported.explanation: Correct option: B (Satisfactory APERs for the three preceding years.).

imported.question: What is the minimum period spent on a grade to be considered for promotion (General Rule)?
imported.explanation: Correct option: B (Two years.).

imported.question: What is the exception to the minimum period requirement?
options: Accelerated promotion exercises approved by FCSC.
imported.explanation: Correct option: C (Accelerated promotion exercises approved by FCSC.).

imported.question: What is the primary method of promotion?
imported.explanation: Correct option: B (Written examination and interview.).

imported.question: Who conducts promotion examinations for GL. 07 and above?
imported.explanation: Correct option: B (Federal Civil Service Commission.).

imported.question: Who is responsible for issuing the result of the promotion examination?
imported.explanation: Correct option: C (Federal Civil Service Commission.).

imported.question: When does a promotion take effect?
options: Date specified in the promotion letter.
imported.explanation: Correct option: C (Date specified in the promotion letter.).

imported.question: Can an Officer refuse a promotion?
options: Yes, but they may not be considered for further promotion for a specified period.
imported.explanation: Correct option: B (Yes, but they may not be considered for further promotion for a specified period.).

imported.question: What happens if an Officer refuses a promotion?
options: Not considered for further promotion for two (2) years except on application to FCSC.
imported.explanation: Correct option: C (Not considered for further promotion for two (2) years except on application to FCSC.).

imported.question: What is "acting appointment"?
options: Temporary assignment to perform duties of a higher post.
imported.explanation: Correct option: B (Temporary assignment to perform duties of a higher post.).

imported.question: Under what circumstances can an Officer act in a higher post?
options: Substantive holder absent for prolonged period or temporary vacancy.
imported.explanation: Correct option: B (Substantive holder absent for prolonged period or temporary vacancy.).

imported.question: Maximum period an Officer can act in a higher post without special approval?
imported.explanation: Correct option: B (Six months.).

imported.question: What happens if acting appointment exceeds six months without approval?
imported.explanation: Correct option: B (It ceases to have effect.).

imported.question: What does "transfer" mean?
options: Permanent movement from one Ministry to another of same status or grade.
imported.explanation: Correct option: B (Permanent movement from one Ministry to another of same status or grade.).

imported.question: Can an Officer be transferred without consent?
options: Yes, but normally only after consultation with officer and MDAs.
imported.explanation: Correct option: B (Yes, but normally only after consultation with officer and MDAs.).

imported.question: Who approves the transfer of an Officer?
imported.explanation: Correct option: C (The Federal Civil Service Commission.).

imported.question: What happens to seniority upon transfer?
options: Seniority in former Ministry shall be maintained in the new Ministry.
imported.explanation: Correct option: B (Seniority in former Ministry shall be maintained in the new Ministry.).

imported.question: What are "inter-cadre transfers"?
options: Movement from one cadre to another on the same grade.
imported.explanation: Correct option: B (Movement from one cadre to another on the same grade.).

imported.question: Who approves inter-cadre transfers?
imported.explanation: Correct option: C (The Federal Civil Service Commission.).

imported.question: What is "secondment"?
options: Temporary transfer of an Officer to another Service or Organisation.
imported.explanation: Correct option: B (Temporary transfer of an Officer to another Service or Organisation.).

imported.question: What is the maximum period of secondment without review?
imported.explanation: Correct option: B (Two years.).

imported.question: What happens at the end of secondment?
options: Officer normally reverts to former post.
imported.explanation: Correct option: B (Officer normally reverts to former post.).

imported.question: What is "posting"?
options: Assignment of an Officer to a duty station within his Ministry.
imported.explanation: Correct option: B (Assignment of an Officer to a duty station within his Ministry.).

imported.question: A public officer is expected to demonstrate what towards the public service?
imported.explanation: Correct option: B (Loyalty and commitment).

imported.question: Public officers must not put themselves in a position where their personal interest conflicts with their:
imported.explanation: Correct option: B (Official duties).

imported.question: What is the primary purpose of the Code of Conduct for Public Officers?
options: Ensure high standards of public morality and accountability
imported.explanation: Correct option: B (Ensure high standards of public morality and accountability).

imported.question: A public officer who receives a gift in the course of official duties must:
options: Declare it to the appropriate authority
imported.explanation: Correct option: B (Declare it to the appropriate authority).

imported.question: Which of the following is a core value expected of a public officer?
imported.explanation: Correct option: B (Integrity).

imported.question: Public officers are forbidden from engaging in any private business, trade, or profession EXCEPT:
imported.explanation: Correct option: A (Farming).

imported.question: What is the consequence for a public officer who fails to declare assets as required?
options: Sanctions by the Code of Conduct Tribunal
imported.explanation: Correct option: C (Sanctions by the Code of Conduct Tribunal).

imported.question: The principle of 'public trust' in public service implies that public officers should:
options: Act solely in the interest of the public
imported.explanation: Correct option: B (Act solely in the interest of the public).

imported.question: Which body ensures that public officers adhere to the provisions of the Code of Conduct?
imported.explanation: Correct option: B (Code of Conduct Bureau).

imported.question: What is the maximum number of times a public officer can apply for an extension to declare assets?
imported.explanation: Correct option: A (Once).

imported.question: The concept of 'due diligence' in public service refers to:
options: Exercising reasonable care and investigation before taking action
Making decisions based on personal biases
imported.explanation: Correct option: B (Exercising reasonable care and investigation before taking action).

imported.question: Public officers should avoid situations that could give rise to:
imported.explanation: Correct option: B (The appearance of impropriety).

imported.question: Which constitutional document outlines the fundamental objectives of state policy, including ethical conduct?
imported.explanation: Correct option: C (Chapter II of the 1999 Constitution).

imported.question: The principle of 'accountability' means public officers are answerable to:
imported.explanation: Correct option: B (The public and relevant authorities).

imported.question: What is the ethical implication of using government property for personal use?
options: Breach of the Code of Conduct
imported.explanation: Correct option: B (Breach of the Code of Conduct).

imported.question: Public officers are expected to show 'impartiality', meaning they should:
options: Treat all citizens equally without bias
imported.explanation: Correct option: B (Treat all citizens equally without bias).

imported.question: Which section of the Code of Conduct addresses the issue of receiving benefits?
imported.explanation: Correct option: C (Section 11).

imported.question: The concept of 'transparency' in public service requires public officers to:
options: Be open and clear about actions and decisions
imported.explanation: Correct option: B (Be open and clear about actions and decisions).

imported.question: What is the role of the CCT regarding public officers who breach the Code?
imported.explanation: Correct option: B (Adjudicate and impose sanctions).

imported.question: Public officers are prohibited from operating foreign bank accounts to prevent:
options: Money laundering and illicit financial flows
imported.explanation: Correct option: B (Money laundering and illicit financial flows).

imported.question: The 'Oath of Allegiance' signifies commitment to:
imported.explanation: Correct option: B (The Constitution and the nation).

imported.question: The Code of Conduct aims to prevent officers from enriching themselves at the expense of:
imported.explanation: Correct option: B (The state and the public).

imported.question: What is the ethical obligation regarding confidential information?
options: Keep it confidential and use it only for official purposes
imported.explanation: Correct option: C (Keep it confidential and use it only for official purposes).

imported.question: The Code of Conduct is primarily designed to promote:
imported.explanation: Correct option: C (Good governance and public confidence).

imported.question: Which institution provides guidelines and training on ethical conduct?
imported.explanation: Correct option: B (Public Service Institute of Nigeria (PSIN)).

imported.question: The principle of 'objectivity' means decisions should be based on:
imported.explanation: Correct option: B (Facts and merits, not feelings).

imported.question: What is the ethical responsibility regarding public funds?
imported.explanation: Correct option: B (Manage them efficiently and accountably).

imported.question: The 'whistleblowing' policy encourages officers to report:
options: Acts of misconduct, corruption, or illegality
imported.explanation: Correct option: B (Acts of misconduct, corruption, or illegality).

imported.question: The principle of 'public interest' implies that public officers should:
options: Act solely in the interest of the public
imported.explanation: Correct option: B (Act solely in the interest of the public).

imported.question: What is the ethical implication of using government property for personal use?
options: Breach of the Code of Conduct
imported.explanation: Correct option: B (Breach of the Code of Conduct).

imported.question: Public officers are expected to show 'impartiality', meaning:
imported.explanation: Correct option: B (Treat all citizens equally).

imported.question: Transparency in public service requires officers to:
options: Be open and clear about decisions
imported.explanation: Correct option: B (Be open and clear about decisions).

imported.question: What is the role of the CCT regarding breaches?
imported.explanation: Correct option: B (Adjudicate and impose sanctions).

imported.question: In cases involving fraud or dishonesty, which agency must be notified?
imported.explanation: Correct option: C (ICPC).

imported.question: What is the main objective of the Code of Conduct under the PSR?
imported.explanation: Correct option: B (Ensure accountability, transparency, and integrity).

imported.question: Which ethical principle requires avoiding using government property for private gain?
imported.explanation: Correct option: B (Integrity).

imported.question: Which behaviour violates integrity in public service?
imported.explanation: Correct option: B (Receiving bribes for official favours).

imported.question: Transparency in Appendix I means:
options: Open and honest decision-making subject to accountability
imported.explanation: Correct option: B (Open and honest decision-making subject to accountability).

imported.question: The ethical value of accountability implies that officers:
options: Must justify their decisions and actions to the public
imported.explanation: Correct option: A (Must justify their decisions and actions to the public).

imported.question: The term Audit Trail refers to documentation of financial transactions to ensure:
imported.explanation: Correct option: B (Integrity and traceability, ensuring accountability).

imported.question: Primary agency for financial crimes like fraud and money laundering?
imported.explanation: Correct option: B (EFCC).

imported.question: Which body monitors compliance and institutional corruption in public finance?
imported.explanation: Correct option: B (ICPC).

imported.question: Ultimate penalty for serious misconduct resulting in forfeiture of benefits?
imported.explanation: Correct option: D (Dismissal).

imported.question: Agency responsible for receiving and verifying public officers’ asset declarations?
imported.explanation: Correct option: B (CCB).

imported.question: Chief Accounting Officer personally responsible for funds within an MDA?
imported.explanation: Correct option: D (Permanent Secretary).

imported.question: Documentation of financial and procurement transactions ensuring integrity and traceability?
imported.explanation: Correct option: C (Audit Trail).

imported.question: Consequence for a bidder attempting to influence the evaluation committee?
imported.explanation: Correct option: C (Blacklisting from future federal tenders).

imported.question: Required step if a bidder's offer is non-responsive?
options: Notify BPP immediately and exclude the bid
imported.explanation: Correct option: B (Notify BPP immediately and exclude the bid).

imported.question: Why is the Permanent Secretary ultimately responsible for procurement compliance?
options: Designated the Chief Accounting Officer for the MDA
imported.explanation: Correct option: B (Designated the Chief Accounting Officer for the MDA).

imported.question: System ensuring integrity and traceability throughout the procurement cycle?
imported.explanation: Correct option: C (Audit Trail).

imported.question: Body monitoring anti-corruption and institutional ethical standards (excluding financial crimes)?
imported.explanation: Correct option: B (ICPC).

imported.question: PSR Chapter providing foundation for integrity and accountability?
imported.explanation: Correct option: D (Chapter 15).

imported.question: Mandatory frequency for asset declaration?
options: Every four years, and upon assumption of duty
imported.explanation: Correct option: C (Every four years, and upon assumption of duty).

imported.question: Agency enforcing asset declaration rules?
imported.explanation: Correct option: C (Code of Conduct Bureau (CCB)).

imported.question: Loss of funds must be communicated to which three offices?
imported.explanation: Correct option: B (Police, Auditor-General, and Accountant-General).

imported.question: Agency investigating financial crimes and money laundering?
imported.explanation: Correct option: C (Economic and Financial Crimes Commission (EFCC)).

imported.question: Prohibition on using official info for personal gain is in which instrument?
imported.explanation: Correct option: B (The Fifth Schedule (Code of Conduct)).

imported.question: Maximum penalty for breach of the Code of Conduct?
imported.explanation: Correct option: A (Dismissal, imprisonment, and disqualification).

imported.question: Loss of funds prompt notification offices?
imported.explanation: Correct option: B (Police, Auditor-General, and Accountant-General).

imported.question: Corruption, fraud, and criminal convictions are categorized as:
imported.explanation: Correct option: C (Serious Misconduct).

imported.question: Mechanism documenting financial transactions chronologically?
imported.explanation: Correct option: B (Audit Trail).

imported.question: Audit Trail ensures:
imported.explanation: Correct option: B (All transactions are chronologically traceable).

imported.question: Purpose of Servicom Dashboards?
options: Citizen feedback and service quality metrics
imported.explanation: Correct option: B (Citizen feedback and service quality metrics).

imported.question: Agency for digital fraud in the public sector?
imported.explanation: Correct option: B (EFCC).

imported.question: Why is record-keeping emphasized in CSH?
imported.explanation: Correct option: B (Ensure accountability and continuity).

imported.question: Vital leadership principle for maintaining ethical standards?
imported.explanation: Correct option: A (Accountability).

imported.question: Integrity and Accountability are essential for which cadre?
imported.explanation: Correct option: B (Leadership (Directorate and above)).

imported.question: Concept requiring regular disclosure of assets and liabilities?
imported.explanation: Correct option: B (Code of Conduct provisions).

imported.question: A Directorate leader ensures fund utilization follows which Chapter of CSH?
imported.explanation: Correct option: C (Chapter 4).

imported.question: Audit Trail refers to sequential documentation to ensure:
imported.explanation: Correct option: B (Integrity and traceability).

imported.question: Primary objective of NSPSR?
imported.explanation: Correct option: B (Enhance capacity, efficiency, and accountability).

imported.question: NSPSR aims to build a public service that is:
imported.explanation: Correct option: B (More efficient, effective, and accountable).

imported.question: Role of M&E in implementing national strategies?
options: Track progress, assess impact, and ensure accountability
imported.explanation: Correct option: B (Track progress, assess impact, and ensure accountability).

imported.question: Ethical consideration in negotiation?
imported.explanation: Correct option: C (Building trust and maintaining integrity).

imported.question: Core principle of public service ethics?
imported.explanation: Correct option: B (Accountability and impartiality).

imported.question: What is a 'dispatch book' in registry practice?
imported.explanation: Correct option: A (Ledger of outgoing official correspondence).

imported.question: What is 'program budgeting'?
options: Linking expenditures to programs and intended outcomes
imported.explanation: Correct option: B (Linking expenditures to programs and intended outcomes).

imported.question: Key element of 'accountability' in planning?
options: Clear roles, performance reporting, and consequences
imported.explanation: Correct option: B (Clear roles, performance reporting, and consequences).

imported.question: What is 'results-based planning'?
options: Emphasizes defining clear outcomes, outputs, and impacts
imported.explanation: Correct option: B (Emphasizes defining clear outcomes, outputs, and impacts).

imported.question: Constitutional body collecting and verifying asset declarations?
imported.explanation: Correct option: C (Code of Conduct Bureau (CCB)).

imported.question: Primary objective of Freedom of Information Act?
options: Ensure public access to information held by public institutions
imported.explanation: Correct option: B (Ensure public access to information held by public institutions).

imported.question: FOI Act enhances governance by promoting transparency and:
imported.explanation: Correct option: C (Facilitating accountability).

imported.question: Public Complaints Commission enhances:
imported.explanation: Correct option: B (Administrative accountability and citizen redress).

imported.question: How can an Officer leave the Public Service?
options: Resignation, retirement, termination, dismissal, or death.
imported.explanation: Correct option: B (Resignation, retirement, termination, dismissal, or death.).

imported.question: What is "resignation"?
options: Voluntary termination of appointment by the Officer.
imported.explanation: Correct option: B (Voluntary termination of appointment by the Officer.).

imported.question: How should an Officer tender resignation?
options: By a written letter submitted through the proper channel.
imported.explanation: Correct option: B (By a written letter submitted through the proper channel.).

imported.question: When is resignation effective?
options: From the date specified in the letter if accepted.
imported.explanation: Correct option: B (From the date specified in the letter if accepted.).

imported.question: Can the Government refuse a resignation?
options: Yes, if disciplinary proceedings are pending against the Officer.
imported.explanation: Correct option: B (Yes, if disciplinary proceedings are pending against the Officer.).

imported.question: What is "retirement"?
options: Compulsory withdrawal on attaining prescribed age or stipulated number of years.
imported.explanation: Correct option: B (Compulsory withdrawal on attaining prescribed age or stipulated number of years.).

imported.question: Current mandatory retirement age in Federal Public Service?
imported.explanation: Correct option: C (60 years.).

imported.question: Current mandatory length of service for retirement?
imported.explanation: Correct option: C (35 years.).

imported.question: What is "termination of appointment"?
options: Cessation of appointment by Government otherwise than by resignation, dismissal or retirement.
imported.explanation: Correct option: B (Cessation of appointment by Government otherwise than by resignation, dismissal or retirement.).

imported.question: Under what circumstances can appointment be terminated?
options: When it is in the public interest to do so.
imported.explanation: Correct option: B (When it is in the public interest to do so.).

imported.question: What is "dismissal"?
options: Termination of appointment as a disciplinary measure for serious misconduct.
imported.explanation: Correct option: B (Termination of appointment as a disciplinary measure for serious misconduct.).

imported.question: Who has the power to dismiss an Officer?
options: FCSC or Permanent Secretary/Head of Extra-Ministerial Office as the case may be.
imported.explanation: Correct option: C (FCSC or Permanent Secretary/Head of Extra-Ministerial Office as the case may be.).

imported.question: Salary during interdiction?
imported.explanation: Correct option: B (Half salary.).

imported.question: Condition for interdiction?
options: Disciplinary proceedings for dismissal are undertaken/about to be and a prima facie case exists.
imported.explanation: Correct option: B (Disciplinary proceedings for dismissal are undertaken/about to be and a prima facie case exists.).

imported.question: Salary during suspension?
imported.explanation: Correct option: C (Earn no salary.).

imported.question: Condition for suspension?
options: Where a prima facie case of a serious nature has been established.
imported.explanation: Correct option: B (Where a prima facie case of a serious nature has been established.).

imported.question: Death while in service?
options: Service is terminated by reason of death.
imported.explanation: Correct option: B (Service is terminated by reason of death.).

imported.question: Action upon death of an Officer?
options: Report death immediately to the OHCSF.
imported.explanation: Correct option: B (Report death immediately to the OHCSF.).

imported.question: Documents to forward to OHCSF for deceased Officer?
options: Death certificate, particulars of deceased, and statement of service.
imported.explanation: Correct option: B (Death certificate, particulars of deceased, and statement of service.).

imported.question: Purpose of forwarding death-in-service documents?
options: Facilitate processing of benefits due to legal beneficiaries.
imported.explanation: Correct option: B (Facilitate processing of benefits due to legal beneficiaries.).
